This plant is native to the southwestern region of North America. The genus Homalocarpus is part of the Brassicaceae family, which includes plants such as broccoli and cauliflower. Its classification within the plant kingdom provides insight into its evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species.
uses: Homalocarpus integerrimus, commonly known as the smooth desert-hickory, has been used in traditional medicine by the indigenous people of the southwestern United States. The plant's bark and roots are said to have anti-inflammatory properties and are used to treat various ailments, including fever and rheumatism. Additionally, the plant's leaves and stems are used to make a tea that is believed to have antiseptic and antiviral properties.
